rulings.Case Title: JORGE DAVID VS. CHEVRON USA INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 2, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Plaintiffs¿ motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. Defendants will have thirty (30) days to respond. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE KIRAS et al VS. AMCORD, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 2, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue one (plaintiffs¿ causes of action for false representation and intentional tort) and denied as to issue two (plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages). As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Kiras¿ exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his disease. Defendant failed to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery on plaintiffs asking for plaintiffs¿ evidence supporting their claim that Mr. Kiras¿ exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his disease and that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto. Thus, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs presented evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. As to false representation and intentional tort, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production. Defendant failed to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery on plaintiffs asking for plaintiffs¿ evidence supporting their claims for false representation and intentional tort and that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto. Thus, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND GENE BENNETT et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Oakfabco, Inc.¿S Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 2, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendants Foster Wheeler Energy Corp. and Union Carbide Corp. and Dana Companies¿ joinder in Oakfabco, Inc.¿s motion to dismiss or stay the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Moving party Oakfabco, Inc. failed to demonstrate that South Dakota is a suitable alternative forum because they did not show that all remaining defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
